Image slots carry a shot description with src:null — designed placeholders until Matt supplies photos. code P-04 + node geometry match the live homepage roster. Specs/copy are sample-grade pending Matt's confirmation.
name: NIGHTMARE KART

hero.alt: Front-elevation drawing of the Nightmare Kart cabinet — in-house stand-in until photography

intro.title: CLASSIFICATION
intro.body:
Nightmare Kart is a freestanding indie arcade cabinet built for Wonderville (Brooklyn) — a custom enclosure for an original kart racer developed with LWMedia. The form is a love letter to the uneasy basement-arcade objects of the late nineties, scaled and materialized for a contemporary venue.

Fabricated in-studio over twelve weeks: shou sugi ban panels around a CNC carcass, a faux stained glass marquee, a programmable interactive lighting rig, and a feedback layer that links physical controls to in-game state. The result is an object that reads as both shrine and machine.

columns.name: Cabinet
columns.body: Freestanding upright cabinet. CNC-cut Baltic birch carcass clad in charred cedar panels. 24″ CRT-style LCD, custom marquee housing, recessed coin door.

columns.name: Control deck
columns.body: Two-player layout, Sanwa joysticks, custom resin-cast buttons with embedded LEDs. Float-mounted with actuator rods and return springs for tactile response.

blocks.alt: Control-deck plan — two-player Sanwa layout — in-house stand-in until photography

rows.title: Faux Stained Glass Marquee
rows.body: Hand-cut 3mm acrylic flooded with tinted two-part epoxy and outlined in black foil. Reads as cathedral glass at distance and as resin up close — the uncanny register the cabinet wants. Backlit by a warm bulb the lighting system can pulse on event.

rows.title: Shou Sugi Ban
rows.body: Western red cedar panels charred with a propane torch, wire-brushed back to expose grain, then sealed in raw tung oil. Matte black at a glance and warm-grained in the hand. Holds fingerprints poorly — ideal for a venue object that gets touched constantly.
